page: UpCloud 12xCPU-48GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration allocates 12 shared vCPUs with 12 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. Memory capacity is 48.0 GB, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a 960 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is categorized in the bottom 10% with a score of 788.69. However, multi-core CPU performance scales to average levels, scoring 9381.33 in stress-ng and 6075.0 in Geekbench. Memory bandwidth is average, reaching 1216.05 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 55.3 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic and compression tasks also perform in the middle 50%, with Passmark encryption at 14018.72 and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 88.86 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of this server is its shared CPU allocation, which contributes to the lower single-core benchmark results. Despite this, the 12-core density and 48.0 GB RAM configuration offer balanced resource distribution for multi-threaded workloads. It is suitable for database operations, static web serving benchmarked at 10.55 GB/sec, software compilation, and light language model inference, where multi-core scaling mitigates the single-core performance limitations.
description: UpCloud 12xCPU-48GB is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 12 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, paired with 48.0 GB of RAM and a 960 GB local SSD. While single-core CPU benchmarks place the instance in the bottom 10%, its multi-core performance is average, as demonstrated by a Geekbench multi-core score of 6075.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 22554.72. Memory bandwidth and data processing speeds, such as static web serving at 10.55 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 17.59 million operations per second, consistently rank in the middle tier. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and web serving, where resource density compensates for shared CPU limitations.
og_description: UpCloud 12xCPU-48GB is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, delivering balanced multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud 12xCPU-48GB is a general-purpose KVM server with 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and a 960 GB SSD for balanced multi-core workloads.
tagline: A balanced multi-core virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.

48.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Average multi-core performance with 6075.0 Geekbench score
Suited for database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded tasks
